of the businesses that you sold, what were the most popular amongst the people who took them on and what were the most successful? I guess beyond that, if you were in the opportunity business now, what businesses would you consider to be the best to go after? Well, some of you may not like my answer, but if I were to go back in today and I would love to, I just don't have stamina to do it. My mother trained me to do it right when you do it. And if I do it right, it takes 12 or 14 hours a day. And I got about three hours a day that I'm worth a damn. So it doesn't work as well as it did. But if I were to go in business today, do it exactly the way I'll do it exactly what I did. I'll go to all the magazines. I'll pick out the ones that I think are any good. I will order their program. I will copy it and put it together. Because why? I started to say this a while ago and somebody very rudely cut me off, I think. I believed and I do believe that when that woman pays $10,000 to business or the ones that don't, the reason is the wife said, John, we're not going to pay $10,000 to that business. Because if it isn't successful, we're in a hole. But when I said to them, I have 43 different businesses you can run from your home with a computer. And if one of them doesn't work for you, you can trade it in for another one. Now, I got scared with that one because I know someone will get all 42 of them over a period of time. So I added $50 to it. And I told them in my cassette tape why I'm doing it. I said, I don't want you to order just to get them. And I'm afraid some of you will. So I'm asking you to send in $50 with every time you change your mind. So you'll have to pay at least something in order to get the program. I don't want your $50, believe me. But I want you to have something involved in it. And it worked. Very few people take advantage of it. But they took advantage of it, but not without sending the $5. So we eliminated or basically eliminated the scaredness of $10,000 and nothing. My answer to them was if you can't do one of these 42 businesses, I said this in the tape, if you can't do one of these 42 businesses, I suggest you keep your regular job because you probably should be in business. If you can't make one of those 42 work for you, I really don't think you ought to be in business. And I believed it. I still believe it. An analogy to that, if you so. So my point to you is. Wait a minute. Just remember your point. So I want to give another example. So the analogy. So when Jeff came to my seminar, he was selling to financial planners one way to build a practice. And the refund rate was astronomical, and the business was not successful. And the thing he was selling them was how to use the telephone and get people to book appointments and come into the office, which everybody wants to result. They want more clients. They want more appointments. They didn't like that shovel when they saw that shovel. And so my suggestion to him simply was, well, let's give them 101 shovels. There's bound to be one they're going to like. Do you know 101 ways to get a client into the office instead of one? Exact same principle. And so a cafeteria line is better than one thing, almost always. OK, now your point. Oh, go ahead. I don't remember. OK. I guess the first part of the question is, what did you see people finding most popular, and also what actually turned out to be most successful? Most popular, I can possibly answer. I really can't answer because I don't really know what was. The monogramming was very successful. We had a monogram machine and we taught them how to monogram. I had a lady in there that was really good. And she would work with them on how to monogram, et cetera. So the monogramming machine was good. It worked well for me. That was, remember, 15 years ago. Why do you ask? Why does that interest you? I'm interested in what people are finding most desirable, what's attracting people, what kind of. I know what my answer question was. I now know what the answer is. How I did it is I just simply called and got all the programs that were out there. And then I bought the ones that I thought I could do. And then I changed them so that I could do them. So I literally didn't invent anything. I copied everything. I don't think I've ever had an original idea in my life. Why? You all have thought it already. If I just copy yours, I can do it all the heck out faster. So I don't think I've ever had an original idea. I really don't think I've ever had an original idea. Maybe one, but I don't think so. I'm a very easy man. I take the easiest way or try to do it anyway. What most of the programs had in common is not unlike what you saw with Go By The Edge. They were things that people could easily, A, see themselves doing and somebody wanting the end result product of. So it didn't stretch anybody's imagination. The bi-weekly was also one of the big times. It was very popular at the time. Bi-weekly mortgage. Help people pay their mortgage off twice as fast. There were several major players in that field. Dave was a major player in that field. And so again, he copied the program, plugged it into their computer program, and there it went. But I would not lose the importance of the 4 to 6 to 8 to 12 to 42. That was brilliant. And the umbrella, of course, is at home with your computer. So even though people didn't have one, they knew what one was. And they were getting one, which was some added appeal. Because you're not only getting put in business, you're getting.
